column1: Luis Arraez stands out as the top pick, offering exceptional reliability with a hit in over 76% of games and a favorable platoon matchup against Ryan Gusto. Spencer Horwitz also brings a strong outlook in the leadoff spot with a park boost and a soft righty on the mound, though his recent consistency lags behind Arraez. Bryan Reynolds and Brandon Lowe are viable alternatives in similar advantageous matchups but carry less steady hit rates.
